title: Korean Chicken Stew
subtitle: with Kimchi & Lime
description: A spicy, savory Korean-inspired chicken and vegetable stew over cold rice — built for meal prep with kimchi, fried eggs, and a lime finish.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into chunks
medium daikon radish, peeled and cubed
jalapeño or serrano pepper, sliced into rings
eggs, fried fresh at serving time

steps.title: Cook and refrigerate rice
steps.text: Cook 1 cup of white rice according to package instructions. Spread out and let cool completely, then refrigerate — ideally overnight.
steps.note: Cold, dry rice is essential — it reheats better and doesn't go mushy.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics
steps.text: Turn the Instant Pot to Sauté and heat sesame oil (½ tbsp). Add:
steps.note: Cook about 2 minutes until intensely fragrant.

steps.title: Sear chicken
steps.text: Add the chicken thighs (2.3 lb) and sear for 2–3 minutes, stirring a few times.

steps.title: Build the stew
steps.text: Pour in chicken broth (½ cup) and scrape up anything stuck on the bottom. Stir in:

steps.title: Pressure cook
steps.text: Lock the lid and pressure cook on High for 8 minutes. Let pressure naturally release for 5 minutes, then quick release the rest.

steps.title: Reduce the sauce
steps.text: Turn Sauté back on and simmer uncovered for 7–9 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce reduces into a thick glaze.
steps.note: With kimchi adding extra liquid, give it the full 9 minutes if needed.

steps.title: The finisher
steps.text: Turn the Instant Pot completely off. While still piping hot, stir in:
steps.note: Taste and adjust — with kimchi in the mix you may not need the full lime.

steps.title: Pack the containers
steps.text: Divide cold rice across 3 containers. Spoon stew equally over the rice. Top each with:
steps.note: Seal immediately and refrigerate or freeze.

steps.title: Reheat & fry egg at serving time
steps.text: Reheat the container in the microwave. While it heats, fry 1 egg per bowl to your liking — runny yolk is best, it breaks over the stew like a sauce. Slide the egg on top and serve immediately.
steps.note: Don't pack the egg in — always fry it fresh.

notes.title: Kimchi
notes.body: Split it: half goes into the braise for depth, half stays fresh as a topping for brightness and crunch.

notes.title: Lime
notes.body: Goes in off-heat with the finishing garlic. Start with half a lime — the kimchi already brings acidity.

notes.title: Storage
notes.body: Fridge up to 4 days, freezer up to 3 months. Always fry the egg fresh at serving time.

notes.title: Heat
notes.body: Dial the jalapeño and gochugaru up or down to preference.
